title: Roasted American Vegetable Hash
description: A savory and crunchy American dish featuring stir-fry vegetables, perfect for meal prep and anti-inflammatory diets. This recipe is easy to make and can be served at any time of the day.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the sweet potatoes, brussels sprouts, red onion, red bell pepper, and carrots with olive oil, salt, black pepper, and turmeric until they are evenly coated.
Spread the vegetables in a single layer on a large baking sheet.
Roast the vegetables in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the baking sheet from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Use a spatula to scrape the roasted vegetables into a large bowl.
Use a fork to mash the vegetables slightly, creating a hash.
Serve the roasted American vegetable hash hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
